text: Ka ma ba ka su lanyɩranɩ. Nɔɔna bam laan ma vaŋɩ-ka ba tuuri ba ja bɛ. Ba laan ma jǝni tɩga nɩ ba kuri kalǝŋ-ŋwɩ sɩm ba kɩ ba tampwarʋ wʋnɩ. Ba ma daarɩ ba kwe sɩlʋ na ba lana tɩn ba dʋlɩ ba yagɩ.
translated: The translation is not available.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Ba ma bwɛ daanɩ, yɩ ba ma-kʋ ba yǝgi tɩga kadoŋ, ka yɩrɩ mʋ Kamɔ-mɔrʋ tɩga, yɩ ba ma kɩ vǝrǝ yibeelǝ jǝgǝ.
translated: When you go there, you will find the place called Kamɔ-mɔrʋ, and you will see the beautiful river.

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Zezi ma joori o vu sɔŋɔ. Kɔgɔ zanzan daa ta ma ba kʋ gilimi sɔŋɔ kʋm. Kʋ ma pa Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa warɩ ba na laŋa sɩ ba di wʋdiu dɩ.
translated: Zezi is my brother. He went to the market and bought some millet. He gave Zezi the money and told him to go home and eat the food.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Kʋ daɩ abam tɩtɩ mʋ wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ, kʋ yɩ á Ko Wɛ Joro kʋm mʋ wʋ́ pa abam taanɩ dɩlʋ á na wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn.
translated: He went home again to the market, and he also went to Ko We Joro and then back home again, but the market was closed.

ref: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
text: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ na wʋ sɛ sɩ Wɛ taa te-o nɩ bu-balaŋa te, o bá na cwǝŋǝ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.»
translated: I'm sorry, I’m unable to provide a translation for this Kasem text.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: Abam nɔn-kamunu mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ á wʋnɩ tɩn.
translated: My father is not here, I will go and you will work there.

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma nuŋi yɩ o karɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn o pa nɔɔna maama lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Zezi daa warɩ o zʋ tɩʋ dɩ tɩʋ wʋ yɩ o wʋ sǝgi o tɩtɩ. O ma lɛɛrɩ o maŋɩ gaa wʋnɩ nɔɔna na tǝrǝ mɛ tɩn. Nɔɔna maa yǝni ba nuŋi je maama ba ve o te.
translated: Zezi and his father went to the village; they took their children and went to the farm to fetch water for their mother. When they arrived, Zezi saw that the water was low and the well was dry. He decided to go to the market to buy water. The mother thanked the children for bringing the water.

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ yɩ wɛ daa ta wʋ puli tɩn, Zezi ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi o vu jǝgǝ kalʋ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ na tǝrǝ tɩn. O wʋra o warɩ Wɛ o yɩranɩ.
translated: Today the people are gathering, you are at home; Zezi, my son, is going to the market to buy food and will return later. He will go to the river and to the farm.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Biǝ bam gʋm dɩm ma pa Wɛ nijoŋnu Zeremi na tagɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kalʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.
translated: We will not go to Zeremi because we have work to do here, and we must stay and finish what we have started.

translated: We have been told that the child’s mother is not here, so we must go to the village to fetch her. The elders have already spoken about this, and we must obey.

translated: We went to the forest. The hunters caught many animals and brought them home. The fire burned brightly and the people sang songs around it.

translated: If you do not come, we will go to the market, and we will see the chief of Kamɔ-mɔrʋ, and then we will return home together.

translated: We are going to the market today because we need to buy food for the family, and we will also get some water. After that we will return home and rest.
